medications.indication_rationale: Alpha-adrenergic decongestants increase urethral sphincter tone and are a recognised precipitant of acute retention in men with underlying bladder outlet obstruction; should be stopped immediately.

Commonly initiated after catheterisation to relax prostatic smooth muscle and support a trial without catheter; should be started by the clinician performing the catheterisation, not prescribed sight-unseen via telehealth.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop the decongestant cold/flu medication immediately.

patient_education_points.point: Your bladder has become very over-full and can't empty on its own, most likely because of an enlarged prostate made worse by the cold medication you started — this needs a tube (catheter) placed to drain it, which can only be done in person.
Please arrange to be seen today — an urgent care clinic or Emergency Department — for catheter placement; this shouldn't wait until tomorrow given how full your bladder is.
Stop taking the cold and flu tablets you started, as they likely triggered this.
If you develop a fever, chills, or worsening back pain, this could mean your kidneys are being affected — go to the Emergency Department immediately if that happens.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person review for catheterisation
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Post-catheterisation: renal function, trial without catheter timing, initiation of alpha-blocker therapy, urology referral for BPH
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Fever, chills, or worsening back pain — go to the Emergency Department immediately.

must_include_items: Urgent same-day in-person review for catheter decompression
Explicit statement that the bladder needs draining and this can't be done remotely
Safety-netting for fever/back pain suggesting renal involvement
acceptable_alternatives: Emergency Department or urgent care/GP with catheterisation capability are both acceptable, depending on availability
errors_of_omission: No urgent in-person referral for catheterisation
No instruction to stop the decongestant
errors_of_commission: Suggesting home remedies to try to void
Prescribing an alpha-blocker via telehealth without arranging catheterisation first
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Any response that does not include urgent same-day in-person catheterisation is a significant failure given the duration and severity of retention and risk of renal impairment.
